name: Berducedo to Buspol
region_intro: Hiking around Allande offers access to remote mountain scenery and diverse landscapes within Asturias, Spain. The region is characterized by challenging trails that traverse historical sites and significant mountain passes. Hikers can explore varied terrain, from gentle slopes to more uneven sections with notable elevation changes. The area provides a combination of natural beauty and cultural heritage, including traditional villages. Best hiking trails around Allande The most popular hiking route is Camino Primitivo, Ruta de Los Hospitales, Alto de La Marta, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail that takes 6 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route features remote mountain scenery and historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pomar de las Montañas — circular desde Besullo, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.0 km) path. This circular route starts from the village of Besullo and offers views of the surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Berducedo to Buspol, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Asturian countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Allande is defined by remote mountain scenery, historical Camino de Santiago sections, and significant mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Allande are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Allande's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Allande?
A: Allande offers over 20 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult trails, with a few easier options as well.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Allande?
A: Yes, Allande features several circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239498159/pomar-de-las-montanas-circular-desde-besullo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pomar de las Montañas — circular desde Besullo&lt;/a&gt;, which starts from the picturesque village of Besullo and offers scenic views of the surrounding landscapes. Another moderate loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Inn Bar Restaurant – Buspol Pass loop from Peña Bernaz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Allande?
A: Hiking in Allande is characterized by remote mountain scenery and varied terrain. You'll encounter everything from gentle, steady slopes, particularly on the southern side of Puerto de la Marta, to more uneven and challenging sections with significant elevation changes, especially on the northern side from Villayon. Many trails traverse historical sites and mountain passes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Allande?
A: While many trails in Allande are moderate to difficult, there are a few easier routes that could be suitable for families. These tend to be shorter and have less elevation gain. For specific family-friendly recommendations, it's best to filter routes by 'easy' difficulty on komoot.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking in Allande?
A: Allande's trails often pass through areas rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048586833/camino-primitivo-ruta-de-los-hospitales-alto-de-la-marta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Primitivo, Ruta de Los Hospitales, Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4515893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Fonfaraón Hospital&lt;/a&gt;. You can also visit the charming village of Besullo, known for its traditional granaries and connection to Asturian culture.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural features along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its expansive remote mountain scenery. Significant mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2873224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt; and Puerto del Palo offer stunning views. You can also find beautiful vistas over the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the Navia River, with specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Navia River and the Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Allande?
A: The trails in Allande are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 15 reviews. Hikers often praise the remote mountain scenery, the sense of wilderness, and the unique combination of natural beauty and cultural heritage found along routes like the Camino Primitivo sections.

Q: Are there any trails that are part of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Allande is home to sections of the historic Camino Primitivo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048355790/camino-primitivo-hospitales-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Primitivo: Hospitales Route&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging but rewarding hike that offers a unique blend of natural beauty and pilgrimage history.

Q: Can I find any lakes or reservoirs near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the area features several water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, with several viewpoints offering scenic vistas. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2873219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7735890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilotuerto Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Allande?
A: Allande offers diverse hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for exploring. Summer can be warm but is suitable for higher altitude trails. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear due to snow and colder conditions, especially in the higher mountain passes.

Q: Are there any easier routes for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous walk?
A: While Allande is known for its more challenging trails, there are approximately 5 easier routes available. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them suitable for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed outdoor experience. You can filter by 'easy' difficulty on komoot to find these options.
tips: The Romanesque church of Santa María de Berducedo dates from the 14th century and has a beautiful portico with a slate roof. Formerly it was a hospital for pilgrims, because the Primitive Way of Santiago passes through here.

Built with the characteristic local slate, it is a good example of popular religious architecture in the region. It underwent several renovations over time, the oldest part being the one that corresponds to the quadrangular apse and the single nave.

It is a 14th century chapel, with one of the oldest bells in Asturias.
The altar has images of Santa Marina, Santa María Magdalena and San Antonio.

waypoints.name: Church of Saint Mary of Berducedo
Church of Santa María Magdalena de La Mesa
